reviews.title: Great Battery Life, Comfortable hold for a slim-line mouse, Llama is AWESOME!
reviews.text: I'm not crazy about the aerodynamic mice, but I bought one because of the Llama.  It is VERY slim, but more comfortable than I imagined it would be to hold.  The Llama image is on the mouse solidly, it won't come off, which is ideal.  The mouse was easy to install, took my computer maximum of 1 minute to install the driver and my computer is 7 years old.  The other great thing is it has a 'battery saving' feature that puts the mouse into sleep mode when not in use.  To use it again simply left-click once and your mouse is awake and functioning again.  I use this mouse all day for work and at night for pleasure, haven't replaced the batteries yet!

reviews.title: Very good price-point for Laptop portability and Features
reviews.text:
Rating this PINKCAT wireless mouse is difficult in terms of "stars". The price-point and your intended use are big factors.

I'll get one thing out of the way to save you some time - if you're a gamer, I would say that this is not going to have the stable accuracy you need. In fact, I think it will frustrate gamers, so if you're a gamer then you need to move on/up to something more expensive. Just my opinion.

My usage is for work. Lots and lots of work - like 12-15 hours a day of non-stop "click click click" work, so I'm basing my review on that and in comparison, to my main "workhorse" mouse.

My main mouse is a Logitech Anywhere MX, which is an incredible mouse but is also about $50 more than this PINKCAT mouse. I wanted to find something similar in both size and features as my more expensive mouse but with silent click and more as a backup, and for basic uses this fits the bill pretty well.

As you can see in the photos, this mouse is only slightly larger than a Logitech Anywhere MX, so it's still plenty small enough for laptop / portable / small space use. Now that you know what I'm comparing it to, I'll get right to the pros & cons.

PROS:

- Very light-weight, significantly lighter than my MX, yet still similar in size & shape.

- The "silent" buttons are truly that! Something I wish was a feature with my noisy MX buttons.

- Only requires one AA battery (my MX requires two) and excellent battery life.

- Scroll-wheel is smooth, quiet, and comfortable.

- The dull mat style surface is very comfy and doesn't get sticky like the shiny slick surface of my MX.

- The "back" and "forward" thumb-buttons work as expected for quick switching back & forth on web pages and tabs / locations in file explorer folders.

CONS:

- Even with the "3 Grade Adjustable DPI" function, I wouldn't recommend this mouse for gaming or fine digital artwork in apps like Photoshop.

- The signal and receiver seem to be a little easily susceptible to signal issues and occasional jitter, but that could be due to the number of various devices I have in the room. When I first tested it out there seemed to be a lot of jitter and the cursor jumped around a bit, but after switching it to a different system than the one I used my MX on, and adjusting the mouse settings a bit in Windows, it has been pretty smooth.

- No side-to-side push with the scroll wheel, unlike my MX. (But again, we're talking $8 compared to $58).

- Battery compartment door is flimsy. It's OK, but handle with care when popping it open.

Overall, this mouse is fine for basic use, and for its price-point it's excellent.

This was really hard to rate in terms of "stars" , especially coming from the perspective of someone who owns a more finely tuned & designed expensive mouse. So... for the price and for basic use I'd give it 5 stars, but for smooth accuracy it's not going to measure-up to something like a high-end Logitech and would be more like 3 stars in that aspect. But for 8 bucks It's a great mouse - gets the job done for standard computer uses, and it's definitely light, silent buttons! So over all I have to give it 4 stars.

reviews.title: you get what you pay for
reviews.text: these work but feel like crap.  Left and right click are terrible.  Sometimes when I left click, as the piece of plastic is coming back up, it goes under the right side.  Its like someone took a solid sheet of plastic and cut a line down the middle to allow you to press down on the left or right side, its terrible.  Scroll wheel feels fine.  DPI button just cycles through, no way to go up or down, just cycles through 3 modes.  Very uncomfortable.  For the price, you get two and both are exactly like this so I doubt its defective.  I only got these to control my streaming PC so its not a HUGE deal, but if youre getting these for daily use - DONT DO IT.  I can hardly put up with these and I use them for maybe 10 mins a day.  Pay 15$ for one mouse.  I honestly feel like I could crush these with my hand - thats how crappy they feel.  They also go to sleep mode after like 5 mins so I have to constantly click it to wake it up, and it ends up opening apps.  Not worth the 15$

reviews.title: Wired + Wireless?  Work for you? Good multimedia controls & phone slot
reviews.text:
Not a fan of wireless accessories (the batteries typically fail without warning, makers want you to be surprised), I made an exception for this pairing model because the keyboard is wired.

PRO
-Ubiquitous AA battery for mouse
-Economical
-Numeric pad
-Multimedia buttons: Volume knob, buttons for mute, pause, previous, next
-A place to put your phone (nice touch!)

CON
-Neither is backlit
-KB not programmable

NEUTRAL
-Non-'clack-y' key design, these are "quiet"
-Some humans will like pink, not widely available, maybe a good gift
-Con (for me) is the wireless mouse

reviews.title: Fun cordless mouse and keyboard

That gives me more USB ports available for other things. It works great as long as the keyboard and mouse are close to the laptop or the tower. It lags a bit when it’s in a keyboard drawer. I like the compact and portable nature this setup includes!

Easily plugged right into my all-in-one computer.  Took me a little while to get used to the punk keycaps, and now I love how quiet and comfortable they are to use.  The mouse fits well in my hand, and the wheel rolls back and forth smoothly. Top 5 stars for this one!

reviews.title: Great battery life
reviews.text: I'm still not fully decided about how I feel about this mouse. But, I can talk about what I know so far. The packaging I got the mouse and was really nice, really simple, but easy to open. The mouse was easy to set up, and the RGB looks pretty cool. There is a really cool DPI changer function that makes gaming much easier, however, I can't recommend this mouse for gaming because the ergonomics are pretty trash. I have to cramp my hand like a claw over the top of the mouse and after long periods of use it does start hurting. The surface is also pretty slippery, so I found that my hand slips on the mouse all the time. The tracking however, is great! And I would have to say that biggest plus is the battery life. I got this about 2 weeks ago, charged it once, and it is yet to die! I think the features of this mouse are great, but I would not recommend it. Purely because of the ergonomics, if you are going to use this mouse often, then you probably should go if something more ergonomic.

reviews.title: Basic but functions well
reviews.text: I ordered a ‘LED Wireless Mouse, Uiosmuph G12 Slim Rechargeable Wireless Silent Mouse, 2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Silver)’ for review.  For the price point, this is a basic mouse.  The rotating light strip is a cool looking feature.  It is sleek in design.  It connected well with the dongle to my Surface Pro.  It glides well over a mouse pad.  It is rechargeable, which I prefer to battery operated.  The click of this mouse is quiet. The dongle slides into the bottom and of course it has already been lost.  I prefer the dongle to be stored in a closed compartment on a mouse.  It does not require recharging for several days of moderate use and seems to charge quickly.  Overall, for basic use, this mouse works well.

reviews.title: A very nice mouse.
reviews.text:
I tried to use both of the most recent Apple mouse products with my Macbook Pro and never could get the hang of either one of them. I tried configuring them in the applications folder but never could get it right plus neither one of them was comfortable. One had a ball and the other newer one did not. Usually I really like apple products but not this time. I am happy to report this Logitech worked right out of the box after I plugged in the USB thingy. i didn't even have to set it up. I do like the roller ball. I think this mouse can do more than I know about, but am just happy it isn't changing the page I am on constantly. I got the one that was painted blue and green because it was about 6.00 cheaper than the solid colored ones. I like the design now that I have it. Since my laptop only has two USB plug ins, I bought the Amazon USB hub with three additional spots. Am happy with that too.
UPDATE 11-08-14
All of a sudden I am getting these messages saying the battery is critically low. To start with I was changing the batteries every time but it was happening way too fast so I bought a battery checker. Turns out my batteries are fine. Testing 100 %. the mouse continue to operate even though I continue receiving low battery warnings. Don't know how this is going to play out but it is the first sign of trouble. Now I just take battery out. Test it and when it continues to say 100 % I put it back in and the message goes away for a couple of days. Aggravating. I am amending star count down to 4 stars. Can't change stars.

reviews.title: The middle click and scroll wheel work great! Microsoft 4000 is smoother and more comfortable though.
reviews.text:
I own this mouse as well as the Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 now. I find them to be head to head competitors, so here's a quick comparison.

Logitech:
-Somewhat of a loud click, doesn't bother me personally, but it is obviously louder
-The harder you flick the scroll wheel the more it'll spin. There is resistance though and I feel like it's easier to use than the Microsoft mouse.
-It looks cool and functionality is fine. The middle click is easy to use and the left/right of the wheel to move back or forward in a web browser works and isn't annoyingly used by accident.

Microsoft:
-Quiet click and smoother sliding on the desk
-The wheel stops once your finger leaves it. You can't just flick and go. Scrolling seems more precise with this one, but I actually prefer the Logitech as I feel like I have more functional control with it.
-There is a side button that moves you backward in a web browser, the wheel left and right tilting doesn't seem to do anything.
-More comfortable in my hand.

Bottom line: Microsoft Wireless Mobile Mouse 4000 is smoother and more comfortable, but Logitech is more functional, especially for the middle click and scroll wheel. I was very skeptical about the benefits of this "free-wheel" but I love it now. If you're not a big scroll wheel and middle click enthusiast go with the Microsoft, otherwise the Logitech is for you.

features: 【Stable 2.4GHz Wireless Connection, Plug & Play】2.4GHz wireless technology provides stronger anti-interference capability, faster transmission speed and more reliable connection. 【Strong Compatibility, Slim Portable Size】This COCNI wireless mouse is used in various computer devices (Macbook, Laptop, Desktop etc.) and various computer operating systems (MacOS, Win xp/7/8/10, Linux etc.). The ultra-thin design allows the cordless mouse to be carried anywhere at any time, because it can be easily placed in a laptop box or bag. reviews.title: One of the best everyday mice I've used
reviews.text:
I have input devices for drawing on my tablet, and I have a gaming mouse, but the Logitech M305 is an excellent mouse for every day use, so it's the one I use the most.

The mouse itself has very basic features,
* scroll wheel (mild clicking feedback)with side-click options
* battery status light (green when fully charged, red when almost out)
* two buttons
* an off switch
* a nano USB transmitter than stores inside the mouse
* uses a single AA battery

It does come in jazzy designs, and I liked the yellow/gold one the best. The one thing I might have wanted to change on this mouse is the on/off switch. The best nano-USB mouse I've ever had would automatically turn off when you stored the transmitter inside. With this one, be sure to flip the switch to off as well before you put it in your bag, or it will be down there draining the battery as it moves around.

title: Bluetooth Mouse for iPad MacBook Air Pro Mini Mac iPhone Tablet Cell Phone, Computer Wireless Mouse for Laptop PC Desktop, Dual Mode (Bluetooth + 2.4G), Portable Optical Cordless Mice Silent (Silver)
description:
Specifications:
Bluetooth Edition: Bluetooth 5.0 Mouse Dimensions: 4.21 X 2.36 X 1.26 inchs Mouse Weight: 2.05 ounces Operating Range: Up to 33 ft/ 10 meters Buttons Number: 3 Keys DPI Level: 1600 DPI Battery Powered: 1 AA battery powered,
Battery NOT Included
The mouse supports Bluetooth and 2.4GHz USB connection
Note: The Bluetooth Mouse only compatible with iPad OS/iOS 13 and above. So you need to update the system of your ipad and iphone to iPad OS/iOS 13 and above, and turn on “Assistive Touch”: Setting> Accessibility> Touch> Assistive Touch, then connect the mouse.
How to connect Bluetooth mouse:
1: Turn on the power ON/OFF, switch to Bluetooth. 2: Then Turn on the Bluetooth switch of your tablet or mobile phone, and search for Bluetooth mouse. 3: Click the Bluetooth mouse on tablet phone or other devices to connect to your device.
How to connect 2.4GHz USB mouse:
1: Turn on the power ON/OFF, switch to 2.4G. 2: Then insert the USB receiver into the USB port of the notebook or computer, and then you can use it.
Package Included:
1x Dual Mode Mouse (Bluetooth+2.4GHz) 1x USB Receiver 1x User Manual
Broad Compatibility:
Compatible with iPadOS iPad 10.2/9.7 5th 6th 7th 8th 9th gen, iPad Air 10.5/10.9, iPad Pro 11/12.9 2nd 3rd 4th 5th generation, iPad Mini 5/4/3, iPhone 12 11 pro max, iPhone X XS XR max, etc.
Q&A
Where is the USB receiver?
The USB receiver is in the mouse, you can find it when you open the mouse's battery cover. When you don't use it, you can plug the nano receiver into the USB storage slot again, so you don't lose the USB.
Connectivity and USB troubleshooting
Restart your computer and make sure the mouse is turned on and not too far away from the USB Receiver.
Warranty: 18 months warranty, and our friendly customer service.
Bluetooth mouse only compatible with iPad OS/ iOS 13 and above.
features: Wide Compatibility: The mouse compatible with Windows XP/ Vista/ 7/ 8/ 10/ 11, Chrome OS, Mac OS, Android, iOS, and perfect for laptop notebook PC computer desktop chromebook, Apple iPhone iPad, iPad Air, iPad Mini, iPad Pro, MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, Mac iMac Samsung Galaxy Tab/ Note/ S tablet smartphone Surface Pro and other multi device.【Bluetooth mouse compatible with iPad OS/ iOS 13 and above, turn on “Assistive Touch”: Setting> Accessibility> Touch> Assistive Touch】

Dual-Mode Mouse (2.4G + Bluetooth 5.0): 2.4GHz wireless mode, plug and play, no need to download drivers. Bluetooth mode, easy to connect and pair. Both modes provide a reliable and stable connection up to 33 feet with virtually no drop-outs and delays. The wireless mouse realizes free switches between two devices with one mouse only by one click. Note: You can't use Bluetooth and 2.4G mode at the same time. The USB dongle is stored in the compartment next to the mouse battery slot.

Comfortable & Quiet Click: The wireless mouse features a hand-friendly contoured shape for all-day comfort. Quiet clicks allow you to focus on your work without worrying about disturbing others or causing unwanted distracting noises. 1600 DPI cursor sensitivity provides smooth, precise tracking and scrolling for easy navigation on most surfaces. The sturdy rubber scroll wheel prevents your hands from slipping when rolling. The nano receiver is stored on the back of the mouse.

Long Battery Life: The mouse is powered by 1 AA battery (battery is not included). The mouse will enter into auto-sleep mode after 10 minutes of inactivity, and you can click any key to wake it up. Long battery life and working time, it can be used for months without battery replacement. There is an ON/ OFF button on the back of the mouse, which can be turned off to save power. Battery NOT included.

Protable & Durable Design: The ergonomic design and smooth surface fit your hands perfectly and provide you with a comfortable experience. You can take it anywhere you need it for office, home, meetings or travel. Tested over 3 million clicks to ensure mouse durability. What You Will Get: Wireless Bluetooth Mouse, USB Receiver, User Manual, 18 months warranty, and our friendly customer service.

title: Iron Man Wireless Mouse 2.4G Full Size Wireless Optical Mice with Nano USB Receiver, 3 Adjustable DPI Levels, 3 Buttons for Notebook, PC, Laptop, Computer, MacBook (Gold)
features: Two AAA battery provides more than 6 months of battery life, recommend Polaroid AAA batteries for extended use, and on-off button helps conserve battery life.
Snap-in Nano transceiver stows conveniently in the bottom of the mouse.
Innovative design to provide comfort and control for either hand.
Reliable 2.4GHz wireless connection with up to 33-foot range.

Work perfectly for Windows XP, Windows Vista, Windows 7, Mac OS X 10.4 etc. (Pls Note: this mouse is connected by USB receiver, will NOT compatible with MacBook Pro or other devices which only have Type C ports)

title: cimetech Rechargeable Cordless Mouse, 2.4G Computer Mouse Wireless Optical Mice for Laptop, Slim Quiet Wireless Mouse with USB Nano Receiver (Rose Quartz)
features: 【480mAh RECHARGEABLE BATTERY】Rechargeable laptop mouse built-In an 480mah Lithium ion battery, charge 1 hour and work for 2 months.Save you the cost of buying battery.

【5 ADJUSTABLE DPI】5 adjustable DPI 2400/2000/1600(default)/1200/800, high polling rate 125(default)/250 Hz computer mouse to meet your multi needs. Can easily change the pc mouse sensitivity to match your activity.
【2.4GHz WIRELESS】No driver, Plug & Play. Wireless mouse for laptop provide you with a more secure and precise experience with an increased working distance up to 15m/49FT. Faster response.
【QUIET CLICK MOUSE WIRELESS】Mute quite clicks make you concentrate on your work and free you worry about bothering others.
【SLIM AND PORTABLE DESIGN】Slim mouse ergonomic design and smooth frosted surface provide an incredible comfort fit. It’s easy to put in your laptop bag or pockets. Nice ultra thin appearance.

reviews.title: Good find!
reviews.text: I got this to keep in my work bag as a backup/when I work from home. I will say the first impression wasn't stellar. The top plastic has a cheap look/feel to it. But it also makes up in some nice touches. For example, the USB fob. I was apprehensive about the keep slot for it because there wasn't something that made it feel or look actually secured. But turns out, there's magnetic component so it gets sucked in rather than loosely placed. Battery power seems as advertised. I haven't charged mine in about a month and it's still going. Ergonomics is about what you'd expect based on the photos.

reviews.title: No more Batteries
reviews.text: Love my new mouse! Old one I would turn off and on all the time to save battery. This one I keep wanting to then remember it goes to sleep and no more batteries. First charge went fast, haven't had to charge it since. Love the self storing dongle. Should have purchased this long time ago.

title: LED Wireless Mouse, Wireless Mouse for Desktop Computerr,Wireless Mouse for MacBook pro/air/pc/Laptop,,2.4G Portable USB Optical Wireless Computer Mice with USB Receiver and Type C Adapter (Black)
description: 7 Colorful LED breathing light change alternately, soothing light lights up the mouse in dark, adds more fun to the life. Note: It can't be set to one specific color. How to use it? * Charge the mouse for about 2 hours before using it for the first time. 1. Take the dongle out, which is stored at the bottom of the mouse. 2. Insert the dongle into the USB port of your device, and open the mouse switch (at the bottom of the mouse). 3. Allow a few seconds for installing, no action needed, just wait. Then you can use it. Note: If your computer is Type-C port, please insert the USB-C to USB receiver into the Type-c port of the device first, then insert the USB receiver into the USB-C to USB receiver. Turn on the power switch and the mouse will work. Tips: The mouse cursor will move slowly or stop moving when the mouse is low battery. Please charge it on time. The blue light beside the scroll wheel will be on while charging, and then goes off when the mouse is fully charged.
features: 2020 Latest Upgrade LED Design】The upgraded version mouse with the LED lights adds more fun to the boring office life. The bottom of the mouse has a switch, you can switch lights freely. Low-key or sparkle, switch in one second, suitable for any occasion.

【Plug and Play】No need to install any driver, just plug the USB receiver into your laptop will the mouse start to serve. 2.4GHz wireless technology provides powerful and reliable connection, effective up to 10 meters of range. Advanced optical tracking technology with fast click and responsiveness. 3-Level adjustable DPI (800/1200/1600 DPI), freely to satisfy daily work. Equipped with USB-C to USB adapter, let you worry-free use of any device.

Mute Click & Comfortable Hand-feeling】Silent click Mouse with classic design with anti-fingerprint finish offers your hand maximum support and comfort. Silent button reduces 90% noises, which makes it an ideal wireless mouse for office, conference room, cafe, library, bedroom, help you concentrate on your work, study without disturbing others besides you. Ultra-thin, ergonomic design, lightweight and convenient for carrying or traveling.

Rechargeable Optical Mouse】Built-in durable rechargeable battery, it can be easily charged using the included USB cable without replacing the battery. Just 2 hours charging, you can use it about 7-15 days. Standby time is very long, energy-saving features, automatic sleep mode and wake-up mode are installed to save energy. Turn off the mouse LED light when not in use to save power.

Wide Compatibility】Well compatible with Windows XP, Vista, 7, 8, and 10 / Windows Vista / Windows Vista/ Windows ME etc, Fits for desktop, laptop, PC, Macbook and other devices. Please Note: If your laptop just with type-c port, You need connect the supplied USB-C to USB adapter to your laptop and plug the USB receiver in to it.

title: VELOCIFIRE V9 RGB Gaming Mouse, 9800 Optical Sensor 4 Adjustable DPI with 16.8 Million Color Backlit 8 Programmable Buttons Tunable Weights - Black
features: MUST HAVE - With advanced ergonomics that comfortably support your entire hand. The 8 programmable buttons can be customized with ease to fine tune your play.

ACCURATE RESPONSIVE - Control like you are in the game. Get maximum tracking accuracy from our most responsive optical sensor, minimizes mouse acceleration and increases reliable targeting. Switch DPI modes on the fly - choose from four settings from 100 to 8200 DPI.

BALANCE AND WEIGHT AT YOUR CONTROL - It’s your mouse the way you like it. Customize to your advantage. Three 4.3g weights come with your V9 Mice. Use some or all of them in the positions where you like more weight, or less. This mouse can be tailored to your preferences.
Programmable RGB Lighting - Adjustable RGB lighting lets you select from approximately 16.8 million colors. Use Velocifire Gaming Software to choose lighting color and brightness to match your style.

DPI SHIFT IN-GAME - Be ready in an instant. Be an unstoppable force with V9 Mice in either low or high DPI. Shift from headshot-precise 100 DPI targeting to lightning-fast 8200 DPI maneuvers by shifting through up to four DPI settings per profile.

reviews.title: faulty unit
reviews.text: The right click does not work, it seems to be a design flaw or its just so cheap.

reviews.title: I found the buttons too easy to click
reviews.text: Meh...I found the buttons too easy to click, sometimes just the weight of my hand would make the mouse click. Also, the mouse cursor would wander when the mouse itself wasn't moving. It has some features that surprised me like being able to take weights in and out of the mouse for customized weight, but the easy clicks and the wandering cursor were deal breakers. Overall it feels a bit cheap. I got a logitech 510 instead and really like that mouse. Just a casual gamer here, wasn't looking for anything fancy.

reviews.title: Great Mouse!
reviews.text:
I really like this mouse and for the money, it is by far the best value in a gaming mouse I have purchased to date. I have spent over $100 for other gaming mice just to have them wear out after a year of use. I paid about $20 for this mouse and I am not disappointed in the quality at all. It uses the same switches as more expensive mice, feels great in my hand and is just as responsive as those more expensive models I purchased in the past. You may not get all the RGB lighting effects or programming features, but for the cost it cannot be beat.

The only thing that is a slight disappointment is the scroll wheel. One click is too strong and there are no settings in the software to adjust it. For the price I paid, it is something I can easily overlook and still give this mouse a 5 star review.

reviews.title: Defective Mouse
reviews.text:
This mouse would rate five stars if it didn't go bad after the return date.

It feels nice in the hand, it does all the mouse things it is supposed to, and it slides around smoothly. That is, when it decides to connect to my computer. I have a macbook pro with a bootcamp partition and primarily plug in through a USB hub. At first, I thought the hub had issues, but my keyboard is also connected to the hub and it has zero problems connecting with either OS. The mouse, on the other hand, likes to go on strike, even when plugged directly into the laptop's USB port. I have yet to determine the conditions cause the mouse to stop working, but I have yet to find a pattern. The mouse is just as likely to stop working on El Capitan as it is on Windows 10.

I tried to contact Velocifire since my mouse started being lazy after the return window, but I never heard back. I will not be buying from this company again.
